headline: I Sailed the MV Hondius. Its Crisis Won’t Derail Cruising.
summary: Today, three people have died on board the ship from what appears to be an outbreak of hantavirus, and the MV Hondius is hoping to be accepted by a Spanish port.  In an eerie replay of the early days of Covid-19’s spread, officials say it can’t dock without an inspection and remaining passengers are under quarantine, although the World Health Organization says the broader public-health risk is low.  It’s a new test for an industry that’s jumped from strength to strength in the postpandemic era, but one that’s unlikely to derail enthusiasm for cruises.

headline: Northcoast Just Yanked Norwegian Cruise Line From Its Buy List: Iran War Sinks the Cruise Industry
summary: Northcoast Research has pulled Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) from its Buy list, downgrading the stock to Neutral on May 6. The firm cited a slower-than-expected balance sheet transformation and an industry backdrop made worse by the war in Iran. The analyst downgrade lands one day after price target cuts from Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Barclays, ... Northcoast Just Yanked Norwegian Cruise Line From Its Buy List: Iran War Sinks the Cruise Industry

headline: Oceania Cruises® Launches Two Epic 180-Day Around the World Cruises for 2028 and 2029 Aboard Oceania Aurelia™
summary: Oceania Cruises® has unveiled the inaugural sailings for Oceania Aurelia™, set to debut in late 2027, which include the launch of two epic 180-day Around the World voyages for 2028 and 2029 and two Grand Voyages of more than 70 days each.

headline: Wells Fargo Maintains Overweight on Norwegian Cruise Line, Lowers Price Target to $20
summary: Wells Fargo  analyst Trey Bowers   maintains Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) with a Overweight and lowers the price target from $26 to $20.
